articles.headline: Government Launches National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II, Targets Industrial Decarbonization
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government officially launched Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and demand in hard-to-abate sectors.
• This phase aims to integrate green hydrogen into critical industrial processes such as steel, cement, and petrochemicals, promoting cleaner manufacturing.
• Key incentives include production-linked schemes for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production, along with viability gap funding for pilot projects.
• The mission seeks to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to energy independence and climate goals.
• Emphasis is also placed on developing a robust supply chain, R&D in cost-effective technologies, and skill development for the emerging green hydrogen economy.
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023 with an outlay of ₹19,744 crore.
Phase I focused on SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme.
Phase II targets industrial applications and demand creation.
India aims for 5 MMT (million metric tonnes) of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
The mission is under the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy transition and climate commitments.
Analyze the potential economic benefits and challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate how government policies like PLI and VGF can accelerate the development of the green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the role of green hydrogen in decarbonizing critical industries and achieving Net Zero targets.
Critically assess the infrastructure and technological hurdles India faces in becoming a global green hydrogen hub.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Amendments to Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 Regarding Cross-Border Data Flows
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India upheld recent amendments to the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023, specifically those concerning cross-border data transfers.
• The amendments introduce a 'whitelist' approach for data transfers to trusted jurisdictions, enhancing data sovereignty and national security.
• Petitions challenging the amendments argued potential stifling of innovation and increased compliance burden for businesses.
• The Court emphasized the government's prerogative to balance data protection with national security and economic interests, citing global practices.
• This ruling provides clarity for tech companies and data fiduciaries operating in India, impacting their global data transfer strategies.
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019.
The Act applies to digital personal data processed in India and cross-border transfers.
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
The 'whitelist' approach allows data transfer only to specified countries/territories.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution ensures the Right to Privacy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's decision on India's digital economy and international data governance.
Discuss the balance struck by the DPDP Act between individual privacy rights, national security, and economic growth.
Examine the challenges and opportunities presented by the 'whitelist' approach for Indian and multinational businesses.
Critically evaluate the role of the judiciary in interpreting and upholding data protection legislation in the digital age.
Compare India's data protection framework with global standards like GDPR, highlighting similarities and differences.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel and law books on a wooden desk, symbolizing a court ruling on digital data.
articles.trivia: India is the second-largest internet market in the world, with over 800 million internet users.

articles.headline: RBI Introduces Stricter Regulatory Framework for Digital Lending Platforms to Enhance Consumer Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced a comprehensive new regulatory framework aimed at curbing predatory practices and enhancing transparency in the digital lending sector.
• Key provisions include mandatory disclosure of all charges, strict limits on interest rates, and a cooling-off period for loan repayment.
• The framework also mandates that all loan disbursements and repayments must be executed directly between the borrower and the Regulated Entity (RE), bypassing third-party loan service providers (LSPs).
• This move comes amidst rising concerns over unethical recovery practices, data privacy breaches, and exorbitant interest rates charged by some unregulated digital lending apps.
• The new guidelines seek to foster a safer and more transparent digital lending ecosystem, protecting vulnerable borrowers from exploitation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
prelimsPoints: RBI is the central bank of India, established in 1935.
The new framework primarily targets unregulated digital lending apps.
It mandates direct credit to borrowers' bank accounts from REs.
The guidelines cover all entities under RBI's regulatory purview, including banks and NBFCs.
A 'cooling-off' period allows borrowers to exit a loan by repaying the principal.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity and implications of the RBI's new regulatory framework for digital lending on the Indian financial sector.
Analyze how these guidelines address the challenges of consumer exploitation and data privacy in the digital lending space.
Evaluate the potential impact of the framework on innovation and growth within India's fintech ecosystem.
Examine the role of regulatory bodies like RBI in adapting to technological advancements in financial services.
Suggest further measures that could be taken to ensure financial inclusion while safeguarding consumer interests in digital lending.
articles.imageDescription: Smartphone displaying a digital lending app with a magnifying glass over it, symbolizing scrutiny.
articles.trivia: India's fintech market is projected to reach $1 trillion by 2025, driven by digital payments and lending.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Trade Ministers' Meeting, Focus on Global Supply Chain Resilience and WTO Reforms
articles.explainer:
• India is hosting the G20 Trade Ministers' Meeting, bringing together delegates from major economies to discuss critical global trade issues.
• A primary agenda item is building resilient global supply chains, especially in light of recent geopolitical disruptions and the lessons from the pandemic.
• Discussions also include reforms to the World Trade Organization (WTO) to enhance its effectiveness in dispute resolution and rule-making.
• India advocated for equitable trade practices, support for developing nations, and integration of MSMEs into global value chains.
• The meeting aims to forge consensus on a common framework for digital trade and address non-tariff barriers impeding international commerce.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: G20 (Group of Twenty) is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
India held the G20 Presidency in 2023.
The WTO is the only global international organization dealing with the rules of trade between nations.
Supply chain resilience refers to a supply chain's ability to prepare for and respond to disruptions.
MSMEs (Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises) are crucial for economic growth and employment in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and objectives in shaping global trade policies through forums like the G20.
Discuss the challenges and strategies for building resilient global supply chains in an increasingly uncertain world.
Evaluate the necessity and potential impact of WTO reforms on multilateral trade governance.
Examine how digital trade can be leveraged to promote inclusive economic growth, especially for developing countries.
Critically assess the implications of non-tariff barriers on India's export potential and import dependency.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of G20 nations arranged around a meeting table, symbolizing international cooperation.
articles.trivia: The G20 represents about two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test of Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully conducting a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• The test involved a precise autonomous landing sequence, mimicking a spaceplane returning from orbit, demonstrating advanced guidance and navigation capabilities.
• This experiment is a vital step towards developing India's own two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch system, aiming to drastically reduce space launch costs.
• The RLV-TD program focuses on mastering technologies like hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and powered cruise flight.
• Successful development of RLV technology will enhance India's competitive edge in the global space market and support future ambitious space missions.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched the RLV-TD program in 2016 with its first experimental flight.
RLV-TD is a winged body aerospace vehicle operating in both atmospheric and space flight regimes.
The ultimate goal is to develop a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) vehicle.
It aims to reduce the cost of space launches by 10 times.
Key technologies being tested include hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and scramjet propulsion.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its global standing.
Analyze the economic and technological advantages that RLV-TD development offers to ISRO and the Indian space industry.
Examine the challenges involved in developing and operationalizing a fully reusable launch system.
Evaluate how RLV technology can contribute to future deep-space missions and commercial space ventures.
Critically assess India's progress in indigenous space technology and its implications for national security and scientific research.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's RLV-TD gliding back to Earth for an autonomous landing.
articles.trivia: The first fully reusable orbital spacecraft was NASA's Space Shuttle, which first flew in 1981.

articles.headline: New Tiger Census Report Highlights Continued Growth in Population, Calls for Enhanced Habitat Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The latest All India Tiger Estimation (AITE) report, released today, shows a positive trend with a sustained increase in the country's tiger population.
• While overall numbers are up, the report identifies fragmented habitats and human-wildlife conflict as persistent challenges.
• It emphasizes the critical need for improving habitat connectivity through wildlife corridors to ensure genetic diversity and dispersal.
• The report also highlights the success of various conservation programs like Project Tiger and community participation initiatives.
• Recommendations include intensified anti-poaching measures, scientific management of tiger reserves, and eco-development activities for local communities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: India is home to over 70% of the world's tiger population.
All India Tiger Estimation is conducted every four years.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) is an app-based monitoring system.
The latest previous census in 2022 recorded 3,682 tigers (average), an increase from 2,967 in 2018.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the growth in India's tiger population and the challenges that still persist in tiger conservation.
Discuss the significance of habitat connectivity and wildlife corridors for long-term tiger survival and genetic health.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's conservation policies and programs, such as Project Tiger, in protecting big cats.
Examine the socio-economic dimensions of human-wildlife conflict and suggest sustainable mitigation strategies.
Critically assess the role of technology and community involvement in modern tiger conservation efforts.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through a dense forest, symbolizing wildlife conservation efforts.
articles.trivia: Tigers are apex predators and their presence indicates a healthy ecosystem.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development and Vocational Training
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020's implementation was presented, focusing on its impact on skill development.
• The report noted significant progress in integrating vocational education into mainstream schooling and higher education institutions.
• Initiatives like the National Credit Framework (NCrF) and multidisciplinary approaches are fostering greater flexibility and recognition of skills.
• Challenges remain in teacher training, infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access to quality vocational courses across all regions.
• The review emphasized continued efforts to align education with industry demands and prepare students for the future workforce.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
It aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level by 2030.
The policy promotes vocational education from Class 6 onwards.
NCrF is an overarching framework for seamlessly integrating academic and vocational education.
The target for GER in higher education is 50% by 2035.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in addressing India's skill gap and demographic dividend.
Discuss the key initiatives under NEP 2020 that are driving skill development and vocational training.
Evaluate the challenges in effective implementation of vocational education at scale across diverse educational institutions.
Examine how the National Credit Framework can empower students and enhance the employability of graduates.
Suggest policy measures to bridge the gap between academic learning and industry requirements under the NEP framework.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in vocational training, learning practical skills in a workshop setting.
articles.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations in the world, with over 65% of its population under 35.

articles.headline: Ministry of Home Affairs Unveils New Cyber Security Strategy for Critical Infrastructure Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) launched a comprehensive new National Cyber Security Strategy specifically designed to protect India's critical information infrastructure.
• The strategy emphasizes a multi-pronged approach, including enhanced threat intelligence sharing, capacity building, and public-private partnerships.
• Key focus areas include securing power grids, financial systems, telecommunications networks, and defense systems from sophisticated cyber attacks.
• The plan outlines protocols for rapid response to cyber incidents and mandates regular audits for critical infrastructure operators.
• This move is a response to the escalating global cyber threats and aims to bolster India's cyber resilience against state-sponsored and criminal actors.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: NCIIPC is the nodal agency for protecting critical information infrastructure in India.
The MHA is responsible for internal security, including cyber security policy.
Critical infrastructure includes sectors like energy, finance, transport, and defense.
India faces threats from state-sponsored hacking groups and cybercriminals.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for cyber security incident response.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of cyber attacks on critical infrastructure and its implications for India's national security.
Analyze the key pillars of the new National Cyber Security Strategy and their potential effectiveness in mitigating cyber risks.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in building a robust cyber defense mechanism for critical infrastructure.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a comprehensive cyber security strategy across diverse sectors and technologies.
Suggest measures to enhance India's capacity for cyber deterrence and response in the face of evolving threats.
articles.imageDescription: Digital padlock superimposed on a network of interconnected infrastructure, symbolizing cyber protection.
articles.trivia: Cyber attacks on critical infrastructure have increased by over 200% globally in the last five years.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Ladakh
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery complex in a remote region of Ladakh.
• The excavation has revealed well-preserved stupas, meditation cells, and intricate murals dating back to the 7th-8th century CE, indicating early Buddhist presence.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in the Himalayan region and the cultural exchanges along ancient trade routes.
• Preliminary findings suggest a sophisticated monastic life and artistic traditions in the area, contributing to understanding Ladakh's historical tapestry.
• The site is undergoing further study to ascertain its full historical and architectural significance, with plans for conservation and public access.
gsSubTopics: Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
prelimsPoints: ASI is an attached office of the Ministry of Culture.
Ladakh is known for its rich Buddhist cultural heritage.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
Murals are wall paintings, often depicting Jataka tales or Buddhist deities.
Buddhism spread to Tibet and other Himalayan regions from India, often via trade routes.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding the evolution and spread of Buddhism in India and its neighboring regions.
Analyze how such finds contribute to the reconstruction of ancient Indian history and cultural interactions.
Examine the challenges faced by ASI in preserving and managing archaeological sites in remote and ecologically sensitive regions like Ladakh.
Evaluate the potential of cultural tourism spurred by such discoveries for local economic development.
Critically assess the role of art and architecture in reflecting the religious and social practices of ancient Buddhist communities.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating ancient ruins in a mountainous, desolate landscape, with traditional Buddhist motifs visible.
articles.trivia: The Hemis Monastery in Ladakh is one of the largest and wealthiest Buddhist monasteries in India, believed to have existed since before the 11th century.

articles.headline: India's Chip Design Ecosystem Sees Robust Growth, Attracting Global Investments
articles.explainer:
• India's semiconductor design ecosystem is experiencing unprecedented growth, driven by government incentives and a vast talent pool.
• Several global semiconductor giants are increasing their investments in India, establishing new design centers and expanding R&D operations.
• The 'India Semiconductor Mission' and Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes are playing a crucial role in fostering indigenous chip design and manufacturing capabilities.
• Challenges include bridging the gap between design and fabrication, and developing a complete end-to-end semiconductor value chain.
• This growth is vital for India's aspirations to become a global electronics manufacturing hub and reduce reliance on imported chips.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) was launched in 2021.
PLI schemes offer incentives on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
India has a strong talent pool in chip design, accounting for 20% of the world's design engineers.
Semiconductors are crucial components in almost all electronic devices.
The global semiconductor market is expected to reach $1 trillion by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of building a robust semiconductor ecosystem for India's economic and technological sovereignty.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives like the India Semiconductor Mission and PLI schemes in attracting investment and fostering indigenous capabilities.
Examine the challenges India faces in transitioning from a chip design hub to a significant semiconductor manufacturing nation.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of global supply chain vulnerabilities in the semiconductor industry and India's role in addressing them.
Suggest policy recommendations to further strengthen India's position in the global semiconductor value chain.
articles.imageDescription: Microchips and circuit boards, symbolizing advanced electronics manufacturing.
articles.trivia: A single modern smartphone can contain billions of transistors, the fundamental components of microchips.

articles.headline: Monetary Policy Committee Maintains Status Quo, Reiterates Focus on Inflation Targeting Amid Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest meeting, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to achieving the medium-term inflation target of 4% while supporting growth.
• The decision was influenced by persistent global inflationary pressures, volatile crude oil prices, and uncertain geopolitical conditions.
• The committee reiterated its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating a readiness to act if inflation pressures resurface or intensify.
• Analysts suggest that future rate decisions will be highly dependent on domestic inflation trajectory and global economic developments.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
prelimsPoints: MPC consists of six members: three from RBI and three appointed by the government.
The Governor of RBI is the ex-officio Chairperson of the MPC.
The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The current inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Analyze the impact of global economic headwinds, such as crude oil price volatility and geopolitical tensions, on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in managing liquidity and inflation expectations.
Examine the role of forward guidance by the MPC in shaping market expectations and investor confidence.
Critically assess the transmission mechanism of monetary policy in India and factors affecting its efficacy.
articles.imageDescription: A bar graph showing stable interest rates amidst fluctuating economic indicators, representing monetary policy decisions.
articles.trivia: The first formal inflation targeting framework was adopted by New Zealand in 1990.

articles.headline: India-France Joint Military Exercise 'Shakti' Concludes, Enhancing Interoperability and Counter-Terrorism Capabilities
articles.explainer:
• The latest edition of the bilateral joint military exercise 'Shakti' between India and France successfully concluded in a desert terrain in Rajasthan.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, sharing best practices in counter-terrorism operations, and improving tactical skills.
• Troops from both armies participated in simulated scenarios involving hostage rescue, intelligence gathering, and coordinated assaults.
• 'Shakti' is part of a series of joint exercises between India and France, reflecting their deepening strategic partnership and defense cooperation.
• Such exercises are crucial for fostering mutual understanding, building confidence, and preparing for complex security challenges in diverse environments.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Exercise 'Shakti' is a biennial military exercise between India and France.
Other joint exercises include 'Varuna' (Navy) and 'Garuda' (Air Force).
France is a key strategic partner for India in defense and space.
Rajasthan provides diverse terrain for military training.
Such exercises aim to share expertise and enhance combat readiness.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of joint military exercises like 'Shakti' in strengthening bilateral defense cooperation between India and France.
Analyze how enhanced interoperability and shared best practices contribute to effective counter-terrorism strategies.
Examine the broader implications of India's strategic partnerships with European powers on its regional and global security interests.
Evaluate the role of defense diplomacy in fostering mutual trust and addressing common security challenges.
Critically assess the evolving nature of military training and its adaptation to modern warfare scenarios.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and French soldiers conducting a joint tactical maneuver in a desert environment.
articles.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India in 1998.

articles.headline: South-West Monsoon Update: IMD Predicts Normal Withdrawal, Positive Outlook for Kharif Crops
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) released its latest forecast for the South-West Monsoon, predicting a normal withdrawal across most regions.
• Favorable rainfall distribution during the latter half of the season has led to an optimistic outlook for the standing Kharif crops.
• Reservoirs levels are reported to be healthy in most parts of the country, ensuring adequate water availability for upcoming agricultural cycles.
• However, some isolated regions still face rainfall deficits, prompting concerns for specific crop varieties and local agricultural economies.
• Government agencies are monitoring the situation closely and preparing contingency plans for any localized impacts, especially for Rabi sowing.
prelimsPoints: South-West Monsoon accounts for about 75% of India's annual rainfall.
Kharif crops are sown in the monsoon season (June-July) and harvested in September-October.
Examples of Kharif crops: rice, maize, jowar, bajra, cotton, groundnut.
IMD is the principal agency for meteorological observations and weather forecasting in India.
Normal monsoon is defined as rainfall between 96% and 104% of the Long Period Average (LPA).
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the South-West Monsoon in India's agricultural economy and overall food security.
Analyze the implications of normal monsoon withdrawal for Kharif crop production and rural livelihoods.
Examine the challenges posed by localized rainfall deficits despite an overall normal monsoon, and how they can be addressed.
Evaluate the effectiveness of IMD's forecasting capabilities and their importance for agricultural planning and disaster management.
Suggest policy interventions to enhance climate resilience in Indian agriculture, considering changing monsoon patterns.
articles.imageDescription: A lush green field of growing crops under a partly cloudy sky, symbolizing a good monsoon.
articles.trivia: The word 'monsoon' is derived from the Arabic word 'mausim', meaning season.

articles.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Shows Promising Results, Boosts Early-Stage Innovation
articles.explainer:
• The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) has demonstrated significant success in supporting early-stage startups and fostering innovation across various sectors.
• The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• It has particularly benefited startups in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, promoting a more equitable distribution of entrepreneurial activity.
• Challenges include scaling up funding for a larger number of promising ventures and ensuring sustained mentorship beyond initial grants.
• The scheme is a crucial component of India's broader 'Startup India' initiative, aiming to create a robust ecosystem for innovation and job creation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
prelimsPoints: Startup India initiative was launched in 2016.
SISFS was launched in 2021 with a corpus of ₹945 crore.
It provides financial assistance up to ₹20 lakh for validation of Proof of Concept, prototype development, etc.
It also provides up to ₹50 lakh for market entry, commercialization, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt-linked instruments.
The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) is the nodal department.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of government-backed seed funding schemes in nurturing the startup ecosystem and fostering innovation in India.
Analyze the impact of the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme on promoting entrepreneurship in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
Examine the challenges faced by early-stage startups in India and how schemes like SISFS help overcome them.
Evaluate the contribution of the 'Startup India' initiative to job creation and economic diversification.
Suggest policy improvements to enhance the reach and effectiveness of startup funding mechanisms, including mentorship and market access.
articles.imageDescription: A group of young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern office space, symbolizing startup culture.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world, after the US and China.

articles.headline: India-Brazil Bilateral Trade Sees Significant Growth, Diversification Beyond Traditional Sectors
articles.explainer:
• Bilateral trade between India and Brazil has witnessed substantial growth over the past year, reaching new highs and diversifying beyond traditional commodities.
• While agricultural products and crude oil remain significant, there's a notable increase in trade of manufactured goods, pharmaceuticals, and technology services.
• Both nations are actively exploring new avenues for collaboration in areas like renewable energy, defense, and space technology.
• High-level dialogues and business forums are facilitating greater engagement between Indian and Brazilian companies.
• This expanding economic partnership is crucial for South-South cooperation and strengthening India's presence in Latin America.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Brazil are members of BRICS (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa).
Brazil is India's largest trading partner in Latin America.
Key Indian exports to Brazil include refined petroleum, pharmaceuticals, and chemicals.
Key Brazilian exports to India include crude oil, soya oil, and sugar.
Both countries are part of IBSA Dialogue Forum (India, Brazil, South Africa).
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to the recent surge in India-Brazil bilateral trade and its significance for India's foreign policy.
Analyze the potential for diversification in trade and investment between India and Brazil, beyond traditional sectors.
Examine the role of multilateral forums like BRICS and IBSA in strengthening India's engagement with Latin American countries.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in expanding India's economic footprint in the Latin American region.
Critically assess how increased trade with Brazil aligns with India's broader strategy of South-South cooperation and global influence.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil waving side-by-side, symbolizing bilateral relations.
articles.trivia: Brazil is the largest country in South America and the fifth-largest country in the world by area and population.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Reach, Focuses on Interoperability and Health Data Security
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced further expansion of its services, aiming to integrate more healthcare providers and citizens into its digital ecosystem.
• A key focus is on enhancing the interoperability of health records, allowing seamless sharing of patient data across different healthcare facilities with consent.
• New guidelines were issued to strengthen the security and privacy of health data, addressing concerns over potential breaches and misuse.
• The mission is leveraging technology to create a unified digital health infrastructure, improving access to healthcare and efficiency of services.
• Challenges include digital literacy among the population and healthcare providers, and ensuring robust cybersecurity for sensitive medical information.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched nationwide in September 2021.
It aims to create a digital health ecosystem with unique health IDs (ABHA - Ayushman Bharat Health Account).
ABDM works under the National Health Authority (NHA).
Interoperability allows different digital health systems to exchange and use information.
Data security and privacy are critical components of the mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in revolutionizing India's healthcare delivery system.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with achieving full interoperability of health records across diverse healthcare providers.
Examine the measures being taken to ensure the security and privacy of sensitive health data under the ABDM framework.
Evaluate how digital health initiatives can improve healthcare access, affordability, and quality for all citizens.
Critically assess the role of digital literacy and infrastructure development in the successful implementation and expansion of ABDM.
articles.imageDescription: A smartphone displaying a digital health record interface, symbolizing digital healthcare.
articles.trivia: India has the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme, Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY).

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Releases Stricter Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) Guidelines for Infrastructure Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) unveiled new, more stringent Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) guidelines for large-scale infrastructure projects.
• The updated guidelines aim to enhance transparency, public participation, and scientific rigor in the assessment process.
• Key changes include mandatory post-clearance compliance monitoring by independent agencies and stricter penalties for violations.
• The move comes in response to long-standing demands from environmental activists and judiciary for more robust environmental safeguards.
• These guidelines are expected to streamline the clearance process while ensuring ecological sustainability and responsible development.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: EIA is a process of evaluating the likely environmental impacts of a proposed project or development.
It is mandatory for certain projects under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
MoEFCC is the nodal agency for environmental policy and regulation in India.
Public participation is a crucial step in the EIA process.
Post-clearance monitoring ensures adherence to environmental conditions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and significance of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) in India's development trajectory.
Analyze how the new, stricter EIA guidelines aim to address past shortcomings and enhance environmental governance.
Examine the role of public participation and independent monitoring in ensuring the integrity and effectiveness of the EIA process.
Evaluate the potential impact of these guidelines on infrastructure development projects and the 'ease of doing business' in India.
Critically assess the balance between economic development and environmental protection in the context of India's growth aspirations.
articles.imageDescription: A balance scale with an industry on one side and a lush forest on the other, symbolizing environmental assessment.
articles.trivia: The first national environmental policy in India was adopted in 1986.

articles.headline: India Unveils Enhanced Maritime Security Strategy Amid Growing Geopolitical Tensions in Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• India's Ministry of Defence unveiled an updated and enhanced Maritime Security Strategy, focusing on safeguarding its vast coastline and strategic interests in the Indo-Pacific.
• The strategy emphasizes strengthening naval capabilities, fostering regional partnerships, and robust surveillance of maritime domains.
• It addresses traditional threats like piracy and maritime terrorism, alongside emerging challenges posed by increased naval presence of external powers.
• Focus areas include enhancing maritime domain awareness (MDA), improving coastal security infrastructure, and promoting freedom of navigation.
• This comprehensive approach aims to ensure a stable and secure maritime environment crucial for trade, energy security, and regional stability.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km.
The Indo-Pacific region is crucial for global trade and energy routes.
SAGAR is India's vision for security and growth in the Indian Ocean Region.
Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) includes India, USA, Japan, and Australia.
UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea) provides a framework for maritime activities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving geopolitical landscape in the Indo-Pacific and its implications for India's maritime security.
Analyze the key pillars of India's enhanced maritime security strategy and their role in protecting national interests.
Examine the challenges posed by both traditional and non-traditional threats to maritime security in the region.
Evaluate the effectiveness of regional partnerships and multilateral forums in ensuring freedom of navigation and a rules-based order.
Critically assess India's naval modernization efforts and their contribution to projecting power and maintaining stability in the Indian Ocean Region.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian Navy warship patrolling the open seas, with a map of the Indo-Pacific in the background.
articles.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest of the world's five oceans, covering about 20% of the water on Earth's surface.

articles.headline: India Records Significant Solar Energy Capacity Addition in Q2 2025, Boosts Renewable Targets
articles.explainer:
• India achieved a record addition in solar energy capacity during the second quarter of 2025, significantly contributing to its renewable energy targets.
• The growth is primarily driven by large-scale solar parks, rooftop solar installations, and policy support through subsidies and incentives.
• This surge in capacity is crucial for reducing India's carbon footprint and enhancing energy security, moving away from fossil fuels.
• Challenges persist in grid integration, land acquisition for mega projects, and ensuring consistent power supply amidst intermittency.
• The government reiterates its commitment to achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel electricity capacity by 2030, with solar playing a pivotal role.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's target is 500 GW of non-fossil fuel electricity capacity by 2030.
Solar energy is the fastest-growing renewable energy source in India.
The National Solar Mission (NSM) was launched in 2010.
India is part of the International Solar Alliance (ISA).
Challenges include intermittency and storage solutions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors driving the rapid expansion of solar energy capacity in India and its contribution to climate change mitigation.
Analyze the challenges associated with large-scale integration of intermittent renewable energy sources into the national grid.
Examine the role of government policies and international collaborations in accelerating India's transition to a renewable energy-dominated future.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits of decentralized solar solutions like rooftop solar for rural electrification and energy access.
Critically assess India's progress towards its 2030 renewable energy targets and the remaining hurdles.
articles.imageDescription: Vast solar panels stretching into the horizon under a bright sun, symbolizing renewable energy generation.
articles.trivia: The sun delivers more energy to Earth in one hour than humanity consumes in an entire year.

articles.headline: Government Proposes New Urban Governance Framework to Empower Municipalities and Enhance Service Delivery
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government introduced a draft framework for urban governance reforms, aimed at empowering Municipal Corporations and improving local service delivery.
• The proposed changes include greater financial autonomy for urban local bodies, capacity building for municipal staff, and adoption of smart city technologies.
• The framework seeks to streamline urban planning processes, enhance citizen participation, and promote sustainable urban development.
• Challenges involve overcoming political interference, addressing resource constraints, and ensuring effective implementation across diverse urban centers.
• This initiative aligns with India's rapid urbanization trend and the need for robust local governance to manage growing urban populations and infrastructure demands.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Development processes and the development industry
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
ULBs are responsible for providing basic services like water supply, sanitation, and waste management.
Financial autonomy is crucial for ULBs to effectively discharge their functions.
Smart Cities Mission aims to promote sustainable and inclusive cities.
India's urban population is projected to reach 600 million by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of strengthening urban governance in India's journey towards sustainable urbanization.
Analyze the key features of the proposed urban governance framework and its potential to empower municipalities.
Examine the challenges faced by Urban Local Bodies in India, particularly regarding financial resources and administrative capacity.
Evaluate the role of citizen participation and technology in improving urban service delivery and accountability.
Critically assess how effective urban governance can contribute to achieving the goals of the Smart Cities Mission and inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized cityscape with government buildings and citizen figures, representing urban governance.
articles.trivia: India's urban population grew from 17% in 1951 to over 35% in 2021.

highlights: Government launches Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting industrial decarbonization and energy independence.
Supreme Court upholds Digital Personal Data Protection Act amendments, clarifying cross-border data transfer regulations.
RBI introduces stricter regulatory framework for digital lending platforms to enhance consumer protection and transparency.
ISRO successfully tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator, a key step towards reducing space launch costs.
New Tiger Census Report shows continued population growth, emphasizing habitat connectivity and anti-poaching measures.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. The mission focuses solely on the production aspect, with demand creation being a separate initiative.
3. Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes are a key component to boost electrolyser manufacturing.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The mission aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Statement 2 is incorrect. Phase II specifically focuses on scaling up production AND demand in hard-to-abate sectors. Statement 3 is correct. PLI schemes for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production are key incentives.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is the nodal agency for protecting critical information infrastructure in India?
options: (c) National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) is the nodal agency for protecting critical information infrastructure in India. CERT-In is the national agency for cyber security incident response. NCCC is for intelligence coordination. Cyber Swachhta Kendra is a botnet cleaning and malware analysis centre.

prelimsMocks.question: Regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?
options: (a) It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
(b) It applies to the processing of digital personal data outside India if it relates to offering goods or services to data principals in India.
(c) The Act introduces a 'blacklist' approach for cross-border data transfers to specified jurisdictions.
(d) It replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) The Act introduces a 'blacklist' approach for cross-border data transfers to specified jurisdictions.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement (c) is incorrect. The DPDP Act, 2023, introduces a 'whitelist' approach, meaning data can only be transferred to countries notified by the central government, rather than a blacklist of prohibited countries.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following pairs of Joint Military Exercises and Participating Countries:
1. Shakti: India - France
2. Varuna: India - USA
3. Garuda: India - Japan
Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Pair 1 is correctly matched: Exercise 'Shakti' is between India and France (Army). Pair 2 is incorrectly matched: Exercise 'Varuna' is between India and France (Navy). Pair 3 is incorrectly matched: Exercise 'Garuda' is between India and France (Air Force). Exercises with USA include 'Yudh Abhyas' and 'Vajra Prahar'. Exercises with Japan include 'Dharma Guardian' and 'Malabar' (multilateral).

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a Kharif crop?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Kharif crops are sown with the onset of monsoon in June-July and harvested in September-October. Examples include Rice, Maize, Jowar, Bajra, Cotton, Groundnut, Sugarcane. Wheat is a Rabi crop, sown in winter (October-December) and harvested in spring (April-May).

mainsMocks.question: The National Green Hydrogen Mission holds immense potential for India's energy security and climate goals. Discuss the key objectives of the mission and analyze the economic and technological challenges in its successful implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental Pollution and Degradation; Economic Development
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its significance. Detail its key objectives (production, demand, R&D, cost reduction, export hub). Analyze economic challenges (high initial capital cost, market demand, price competitiveness) and technological challenges (electrolyser efficiency, storage, transportation, R&D gaps). Conclude with policy recommendations and future outlook.

mainsMocks.question: In an era of increasing cyber warfare, the protection of Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) is paramount for national security. Examine the threats posed to India's CII and critically evaluate the measures outlined in the new National Cyber Security Strategy to address these challenges.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management; Cyber Security
mainsMocks.approach: Define CII and its importance. Discuss various threats (state-sponsored attacks, cyber terrorism, espionage, ransomware, supply chain attacks). Detail the new strategy's measures (threat intelligence, capacity building, public-private partnerships, rapid response, audits). Critically evaluate effectiveness, implementation challenges, and suggest further improvements.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's upholding of amendments to the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, marks a significant step in India's data governance framework. Discuss the implications of this ruling for individual privacy rights and the operations of data fiduciaries in India's digital economy.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government Policies and Interventions; Indian Constitution; Judiciary; Information Technology
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the DPDP Act and the Supreme Court's decision. Explain the 'whitelist' approach for cross-border data flows. Discuss implications for individual privacy (data sovereignty, consent, data principal rights) and for data fiduciaries (compliance burden, innovation, market access, global data strategies). Conclude with the balance between privacy, security, and economic growth.

Aryabhata, India's first satellite, was launched on April 19, 1975.

Kaziranga National Park in Assam is famous for the one-horned rhinoceros.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Ladakh
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery complex in a remote region of Ladakh.
• The excavation has revealed well-preserved stupas, meditation cells, and intricate murals dating back to the 7th-8th century CE, indicating early Buddhist presence.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in the Himalayan region and the cultural exchanges along ancient trade routes.
• Preliminary findings suggest a sophisticated monastic life and artistic traditions in the area, contributing to understanding Ladakh's historical tapestry.
• The site is undergoing further study to ascertain its full historical and architectural significance, with plans for conservation and public access.
gsSubTopics: Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
prelimsPoints: ASI is an attached office of the Ministry of Culture.
Ladakh is known for its rich Buddhist cultural heritage.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
Murals are wall paintings, often depicting Jataka tales or Buddhist deities.
Buddhism spread to Tibet and other Himalayan regions from India, often via trade routes.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding the evolution and spread of Buddhism in India and its neighboring regions.
Analyze how such finds contribute to the reconstruction of ancient Indian history and cultural interactions.
Examine the challenges faced by ASI in preserving and managing archaeological sites in remote and ecologically sensitive regions like Ladakh.
Evaluate the potential of cultural tourism spurred by such discoveries for local economic development.
Critically assess the role of art and architecture in reflecting the religious and social practices of ancient Buddhist communities.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating ancient ruins in a mountainous, desolate landscape, with traditional Buddhist motifs visible.
GS1.trivia: The Hemis Monastery in Ladakh is one of the largest and wealthiest Buddhist monasteries in India, believed to have existed since before the 11th century.

GS1.headline: South-West Monsoon Update: IMD Predicts Normal Withdrawal, Positive Outlook for Kharif Crops
GS1.explainer:
• The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) released its latest forecast for the South-West Monsoon, predicting a normal withdrawal across most regions.
• Favorable rainfall distribution during the latter half of the season has led to an optimistic outlook for the standing Kharif crops.
• Reservoirs levels are reported to be healthy in most parts of the country, ensuring adequate water availability for upcoming agricultural cycles.
• However, some isolated regions still face rainfall deficits, prompting concerns for specific crop varieties and local agricultural economies.
• Government agencies are monitoring the situation closely and preparing contingency plans for any localized impacts, especially for Rabi sowing.
prelimsPoints: South-West Monsoon accounts for about 75% of India's annual rainfall.
Kharif crops are sown in the monsoon season (June-July) and harvested in September-October.
Examples of Kharif crops: rice, maize, jowar, bajra, cotton, groundnut.
IMD is the principal agency for meteorological observations and weather forecasting in India.
Normal monsoon is defined as rainfall between 96% and 104% of the Long Period Average (LPA).
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the South-West Monsoon in India's agricultural economy and overall food security.
Analyze the implications of normal monsoon withdrawal for Kharif crop production and rural livelihoods.
Examine the challenges posed by localized rainfall deficits despite an overall normal monsoon, and how they can be addressed.
Evaluate the effectiveness of IMD's forecasting capabilities and their importance for agricultural planning and disaster management.
Suggest policy interventions to enhance climate resilience in Indian agriculture, considering changing monsoon patterns.
GS1.imageDescription: A lush green field of growing crops under a partly cloudy sky, symbolizing a good monsoon.
GS1.trivia: The word 'monsoon' is derived from the Arabic word 'mausim', meaning season.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Amendments to Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 Regarding Cross-Border Data Flows
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India upheld recent amendments to the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023, specifically those concerning cross-border data transfers.
• The amendments introduce a 'whitelist' approach for data transfers to trusted jurisdictions, enhancing data sovereignty and national security.
• Petitions challenging the amendments argued potential stifling of innovation and increased compliance burden for businesses.
• The Court emphasized the government's prerogative to balance data protection with national security and economic interests, citing global practices.
• This ruling provides clarity for tech companies and data fiduciaries operating in India, impacting their global data transfer strategies.
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019.
The Act applies to digital personal data processed in India and cross-border transfers.
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
The 'whitelist' approach allows data transfer only to specified countries/territories.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution ensures the Right to Privacy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's decision on India's digital economy and international data governance.
Discuss the balance struck by the DPDP Act between individual privacy rights, national security, and economic growth.
Examine the challenges and opportunities presented by the 'whitelist' approach for Indian and multinational businesses.
Critically evaluate the role of the judiciary in interpreting and upholding data protection legislation in the digital age.
Compare India's data protection framework with global standards like GDPR, highlighting similarities and differences.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel and law books on a wooden desk, symbolizing a court ruling on digital data.
GS2.trivia: India is the second-largest internet market in the world, with over 800 million internet users.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20 Trade Ministers' Meeting, Focus on Global Supply Chain Resilience and WTO Reforms
GS2.explainer:
• India is hosting the G20 Trade Ministers' Meeting, bringing together delegates from major economies to discuss critical global trade issues.
• A primary agenda item is building resilient global supply chains, especially in light of recent geopolitical disruptions and the lessons from the pandemic.
• Discussions also include reforms to the World Trade Organization (WTO) to enhance its effectiveness in dispute resolution and rule-making.
• India advocated for equitable trade practices, support for developing nations, and integration of MSMEs into global value chains.
• The meeting aims to forge consensus on a common framework for digital trade and address non-tariff barriers impeding international commerce.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: G20 (Group of Twenty) is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
India held the G20 Presidency in 2023.
The WTO is the only global international organization dealing with the rules of trade between nations.
Supply chain resilience refers to a supply chain's ability to prepare for and respond to disruptions.
MSMEs (Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises) are crucial for economic growth and employment in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and objectives in shaping global trade policies through forums like the G20.
Discuss the challenges and strategies for building resilient global supply chains in an increasingly uncertain world.
Evaluate the necessity and potential impact of WTO reforms on multilateral trade governance.
Examine how digital trade can be leveraged to promote inclusive economic growth, especially for developing countries.
Critically assess the implications of non-tariff barriers on India's export potential and import dependency.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of G20 nations arranged around a meeting table, symbolizing international cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The G20 represents about two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development and Vocational Training
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020's implementation was presented, focusing on its impact on skill development.
• The report noted significant progress in integrating vocational education into mainstream schooling and higher education institutions.
• Initiatives like the National Credit Framework (NCrF) and multidisciplinary approaches are fostering greater flexibility and recognition of skills.
• Challenges remain in teacher training, infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access to quality vocational courses across all regions.
• The review emphasized continued efforts to align education with industry demands and prepare students for the future workforce.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
It aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level by 2030.
The policy promotes vocational education from Class 6 onwards.
NCrF is an overarching framework for seamlessly integrating academic and vocational education.
The target for GER in higher education is 50% by 2035.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in addressing India's skill gap and demographic dividend.
Discuss the key initiatives under NEP 2020 that are driving skill development and vocational training.
Evaluate the challenges in effective implementation of vocational education at scale across diverse educational institutions.
Examine how the National Credit Framework can empower students and enhance the employability of graduates.
Suggest policy measures to bridge the gap between academic learning and industry requirements under the NEP framework.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in vocational training, learning practical skills in a workshop setting.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations in the world, with over 65% of its population under 35.

GS2.headline: India-Brazil Bilateral Trade Sees Significant Growth, Diversification Beyond Traditional Sectors
GS2.explainer:
• Bilateral trade between India and Brazil has witnessed substantial growth over the past year, reaching new highs and diversifying beyond traditional commodities.
• While agricultural products and crude oil remain significant, there's a notable increase in trade of manufactured goods, pharmaceuticals, and technology services.
• Both nations are actively exploring new avenues for collaboration in areas like renewable energy, defense, and space technology.
• High-level dialogues and business forums are facilitating greater engagement between Indian and Brazilian companies.
• This expanding economic partnership is crucial for South-South cooperation and strengthening India's presence in Latin America.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Brazil are members of BRICS (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa).
Brazil is India's largest trading partner in Latin America.
Key Indian exports to Brazil include refined petroleum, pharmaceuticals, and chemicals.
Key Brazilian exports to India include crude oil, soya oil, and sugar.
Both countries are part of IBSA Dialogue Forum (India, Brazil, South Africa).
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to the recent surge in India-Brazil bilateral trade and its significance for India's foreign policy.
Analyze the potential for diversification in trade and investment between India and Brazil, beyond traditional sectors.
Examine the role of multilateral forums like BRICS and IBSA in strengthening India's engagement with Latin American countries.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in expanding India's economic footprint in the Latin American region.
Critically assess how increased trade with Brazil aligns with India's broader strategy of South-South cooperation and global influence.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil waving side-by-side, symbolizing bilateral relations.
GS2.trivia: Brazil is the largest country in South America and the fifth-largest country in the world by area and population.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Reach, Focuses on Interoperability and Health Data Security
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced further expansion of its services, aiming to integrate more healthcare providers and citizens into its digital ecosystem.
• A key focus is on enhancing the interoperability of health records, allowing seamless sharing of patient data across different healthcare facilities with consent.
• New guidelines were issued to strengthen the security and privacy of health data, addressing concerns over potential breaches and misuse.
• The mission is leveraging technology to create a unified digital health infrastructure, improving access to healthcare and efficiency of services.
• Challenges include digital literacy among the population and healthcare providers, and ensuring robust cybersecurity for sensitive medical information.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched nationwide in September 2021.
It aims to create a digital health ecosystem with unique health IDs (ABHA - Ayushman Bharat Health Account).
ABDM works under the National Health Authority (NHA).
Interoperability allows different digital health systems to exchange and use information.
Data security and privacy are critical components of the mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in revolutionizing India's healthcare delivery system.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with achieving full interoperability of health records across diverse healthcare providers.
Examine the measures being taken to ensure the security and privacy of sensitive health data under the ABDM framework.
Evaluate how digital health initiatives can improve healthcare access, affordability, and quality for all citizens.
Critically assess the role of digital literacy and infrastructure development in the successful implementation and expansion of ABDM.
GS2.imageDescription: A smartphone displaying a digital health record interface, symbolizing digital healthcare.
GS2.trivia: India has the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme, Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY).

GS3.headline: Government Launches National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II, Targets Industrial Decarbonization
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government officially launched Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and demand in hard-to-abate sectors.
• This phase aims to integrate green hydrogen into critical industrial processes such as steel, cement, and petrochemicals, promoting cleaner manufacturing.
• Key incentives include production-linked schemes for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production, along with viability gap funding for pilot projects.
• The mission seeks to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to energy independence and climate goals.
• Emphasis is also placed on developing a robust supply chain, R&D in cost-effective technologies, and skill development for the emerging green hydrogen economy.
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023 with an outlay of ₹19,744 crore.
Phase I focused on SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme.
Phase II targets industrial applications and demand creation.
India aims for 5 MMT (million metric tonnes) of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
The mission is under the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy transition and climate commitments.
Analyze the potential economic benefits and challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate how government policies like PLI and VGF can accelerate the development of the green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the role of green hydrogen in decarbonizing critical industries and achieving Net Zero targets.
Critically assess the infrastructure and technological hurdles India faces in becoming a global green hydrogen hub.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

GS3.headline: RBI Introduces Stricter Regulatory Framework for Digital Lending Platforms to Enhance Consumer Protection
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced a comprehensive new regulatory framework aimed at curbing predatory practices and enhancing transparency in the digital lending sector.
• Key provisions include mandatory disclosure of all charges, strict limits on interest rates, and a cooling-off period for loan repayment.
• The framework also mandates that all loan disbursements and repayments must be executed directly between the borrower and the Regulated Entity (RE), bypassing third-party loan service providers (LSPs).
• This move comes amidst rising concerns over unethical recovery practices, data privacy breaches, and exorbitant interest rates charged by some unregulated digital lending apps.
• The new guidelines seek to foster a safer and more transparent digital lending ecosystem, protecting vulnerable borrowers from exploitation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
prelimsPoints: RBI is the central bank of India, established in 1935.
The new framework primarily targets unregulated digital lending apps.
It mandates direct credit to borrowers' bank accounts from REs.
The guidelines cover all entities under RBI's regulatory purview, including banks and NBFCs.
A 'cooling-off' period allows borrowers to exit a loan by repaying the principal.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity and implications of the RBI's new regulatory framework for digital lending on the Indian financial sector.
Analyze how these guidelines address the challenges of consumer exploitation and data privacy in the digital lending space.
Evaluate the potential impact of the framework on innovation and growth within India's fintech ecosystem.
Examine the role of regulatory bodies like RBI in adapting to technological advancements in financial services.
Suggest further measures that could be taken to ensure financial inclusion while safeguarding consumer interests in digital lending.
GS3.imageDescription: Smartphone displaying a digital lending app with a magnifying glass over it, symbolizing scrutiny.
GS3.trivia: India's fintech market is projected to reach $1 trillion by 2025, driven by digital payments and lending.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test of Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully conducting a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• The test involved a precise autonomous landing sequence, mimicking a spaceplane returning from orbit, demonstrating advanced guidance and navigation capabilities.
• This experiment is a vital step towards developing India's own two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch system, aiming to drastically reduce space launch costs.
• The RLV-TD program focuses on mastering technologies like hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and powered cruise flight.
• Successful development of RLV technology will enhance India's competitive edge in the global space market and support future ambitious space missions.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched the RLV-TD program in 2016 with its first experimental flight.
RLV-TD is a winged body aerospace vehicle operating in both atmospheric and space flight regimes.
The ultimate goal is to develop a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) vehicle.
It aims to reduce the cost of space launches by 10 times.
Key technologies being tested include hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and scramjet propulsion.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its global standing.
Analyze the economic and technological advantages that RLV-TD development offers to ISRO and the Indian space industry.
Examine the challenges involved in developing and operationalizing a fully reusable launch system.
Evaluate how RLV technology can contribute to future deep-space missions and commercial space ventures.
Critically assess India's progress in indigenous space technology and its implications for national security and scientific research.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's RLV-TD gliding back to Earth for an autonomous landing.
GS3.trivia: The first fully reusable orbital spacecraft was NASA's Space Shuttle, which first flew in 1981.

GS3.headline: New Tiger Census Report Highlights Continued Growth in Population, Calls for Enhanced Habitat Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• The latest All India Tiger Estimation (AITE) report, released today, shows a positive trend with a sustained increase in the country's tiger population.
• While overall numbers are up, the report identifies fragmented habitats and human-wildlife conflict as persistent challenges.
• It emphasizes the critical need for improving habitat connectivity through wildlife corridors to ensure genetic diversity and dispersal.
• The report also highlights the success of various conservation programs like Project Tiger and community participation initiatives.
• Recommendations include intensified anti-poaching measures, scientific management of tiger reserves, and eco-development activities for local communities.

GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) is an app-based monitoring system.
